extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won from four-rider breakaway sprint after TERPSTRA attacked final descent to reduce group, using ROJAS as descending pilot.
tactical_patterns: TERPSTRA attacked final descent to shed rivals, reducing four to trio before being caught, classic Quick-Step attrition strategy.
TRENTIN deliberately followed ROJAS through technical descent rather than leading, exploiting local rider's superior descending to distance others.
Eighteen-rider breakaway required 80km to establish, suggesting peloton indecision or conflicting team interests on transitional stages.
ROCHE gained 29 seconds on FROOME despite peloton finish, indicating Sky lacked motivation to control pace on breakaway stage.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: Second stage win reveals exceptional tactical intelligence to use rivals as pace pilots before decisive sprint.
rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: positive: Initiated winning move on final descent despite cramping later, showing Quick-Step's coordinated breakaway aggression.
rider_signals.ROJAS José Joaquín: neutral: Strong descending intimidated rivals but couldn't convert advantage to stage win when isolated from teammates.
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: positive: Gaining 29 seconds on red jersey group in breakaway stage signals emerging GC threat beyond initial expectations.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost time to ROCHE on controlled stage suggests Sky unwilling to defend lead aggressively on intermediate terrain.
